_about: Curated hook + archetype library shipped with ig-agent. Every row is original or public-domain paraphrase; safe for commercial IG content. Tags drive format compatibility (`any` = universal).

ideas.body_template: Take a contrarian position on a mainstream belief in the niche. Lead with the hot take, back it with one specific reason, finish with a subtle call to reconsider.
Name a widely-recommended niche practice and argue against it. Propose a specific replacement. One-sentence hook, one-sentence reason, one-sentence alternative.

ideas.hook_formula: {thing} is overrated. Here's what actually works.
ideas.body_template: Pick a hyped niche topic, deflate it with specifics, pivot to an underrated alternative.
Produce a {N}-slide carousel. Slide 1 is the hook. Each middle slide names one mistake + the one-sentence fix. Final slide: CTA to save.
{N} rules the creator lives by in the niche. Each as a bold statement + a one-line personal anecdote.

ideas.hook_formula: {N} things I wish I knew about {niche} at {milestone}
ideas.body_template: {N} lessons framed as retroactive advice. Concrete, specific, each tied to a moment in the niche journey.
Rapid-fire {N} truths. Each one beat, ~5s. Hook scene promises the count, final scene delivers the CTA.

ideas.hook_formula: I did {thing} for {N} days. Here's what happened.
ideas.body_template: Document the arc: starting state → 3 middle beats → final state. Specific numbers or visible proof by the end.
Two contrasting states. First scene shows before, second explains the pivot, third shows the result.
Single image post. Visual carries the contrast; caption names the turning point.
Step-by-step how-to. Hook calls out that this is the NON-theoretical version. Slides = steps. Final slide = pitfall warning.
Plain-English explainer. Avoid jargon. Hook promises 60s cap; CTA asks for the next topic.

ideas.hook_formula: The {niche} beginner's guide nobody gave you
ideas.body_template: What every beginner needs to know. Each slide is one foundational truth, written in the voice of someone who has already made the mistakes.
Explain the mechanism behind a well-known niche practice. Short, quotable, one-truth-per-frame.

ideas.hook_formula: I used to {old_belief} — until I learned {new_truth}
ideas.body_template: Personal conversion story. Lead with the prior belief, pivot to the insight, end with what changed in practice.
One regret, framed as advice to yourself N years ago. Emotional but specific.
A single habit + what it changed. Must be concrete — a specific time, action, or ritual.
Two-panel myth bust. Top: the common lie. Bottom: the reality. Punchy, no hedging.
{N}-slide takedown of common niche myths. Each slide: myth → reality → one-line correction.

ideas.hook_formula: What {they} told you vs what {niche} really looks like
ideas.body_template: Side-by-side comparison of marketed expectations vs lived experience in the niche. Each pair = one slide.

ideas.hook_formula: The best {niche} advice I ever got
ideas.body_template: A single quotable line from a mentor or experience, framed as an inheritance.
One-line essence statement. Frames the niche in depth vs surface.

ideas.hook_formula: You're chasing {thing} — but what you need is {opposite}.
ideas.body_template: A paradox the niche community tends to miss. One-sentence flip, one-sentence justification.
Document the experiment with tangible markers. Before/after numbers, photos, or evidence. End with the takeaway.
Milestone breakdown. Slide 1 = the gap. Middle slides = the bridge (method). Final = the outcome + CTA.
Call out ONE specific pitfall. Lead with the visible consequence, close with the one-line prevention.

ideas.hook_formula: Avoid {thing} like the plague if you care about {outcome}
ideas.body_template: Warn the audience about a common niche trap. One concrete example, one reason it hurts, one replacement.

ideas.hook_formula: Everyone asks me this about {niche}. Here's the answer.
ideas.body_template: Pose a FAQ, answer directly, give one deeper-than-expected nuance. Ends with: 'next question you have — in the comments'.
Fundamentals presented as slides. One concept per slide. Language aimed at the absolute beginner.

ideas.hook_formula: If you're at {level}, focus on this next
ideas.body_template: Advice scoped to a specific skill tier. Slide 1 names the level, following slides prescribe the next 3 moves.

ideas.hook_formula: What's your biggest {niche} challenge right now?
ideas.body_template: Direct question to audience. Two-three example replies in the post to prime the response.
Binary poll framing. Sticker a poll on stories. Feed captions can list the two camps.
Take the current cultural trend and apply it specifically to the niche. Keep the reference hot — lean on the trend's native language.

ideas.hook_formula: Everyone's doing {trend}. Here's why you shouldn't.
ideas.body_template: Contrarian riff on a current trend. Argue the niche-specific reason the trend is wrong / overhyped / distracting.

ideas.hook_formula: A day in the life of {persona}
ideas.body_template: POV reel: morning → mid → night beats. Visual-heavy. Minimal text overlay. Real-time feel.
Let the visual do the work. Under 10 words of voiceover total. Proof-of-work framing.
Origin arc in 3-4 beats. Before moment → inciting incident → first win → current state.
Checkable checklist as carousel slides. Each checkpoint is a one-line imperative.

ideas.hook_formula: {niche} levels: beginner vs intermediate vs advanced
ideas.body_template: Three-tier comparison. Each tier gets one slide with 3 distinguishing traits.
React quickly to fresh niche news. Lead with the event, give the one-sentence angle, end with a question.
Single quotable niche truth. Bold, memorable, no qualifiers. Attribution empty or to the page.
Adapt a famous quote specifically for the niche. Keep rhythm and brevity.
Relatable niche-specific frustration as a meme. Top text sets up the moment, bottom lands the punchline.
Expectation-vs-reality meme specific to the niche. Visual contrast carries 70% of the joke.
Multi-stage progression meme. 3-4 panels showing comically escalating states of niche commitment.
Lead with the number. Scene 2 explains the method. Scene 3 the mindset. Close with CTA.
Offer the audience a new way to see a common niche problem. One-line reframe.
Unsponsored-feeling review. Lead with verdict, name one unexpected pro and one unexpected con.
Show the routine as fast montage. Text overlay names each segment. End with why it works.
Setup a situation whose outcome teaches a niche principle. Let the viewer draw the lesson.
Single photo with a hard truth about the niche. Caption expands without repeating the visual.
5-slide defence of a controversial niche take. Slide 1 hook, slides 2-4 arguments, slide 5 direct question to the audience.
Single-panel meme destroying a common niche misconception. Short, blunt, quotable.

ideas.hook_formula: If you only have {minutes} for {niche}, do this.
ideas.body_template: Time-boxed advice. Hook names the time budget, scenes walk through the compressed protocol.
Go one level deeper than the usual niche explanation. One-quote depth.
Curated top-{N}. Each slide: item + one-line reason. Feels personal, not a listicle.

ideas.hook_formula: If you're {niche_identity}, this is for you.
ideas.body_template: Identity-first framing. Declare the audience segment, then deliver a targeted lesson they won't get elsewhere.
Process reveal. Real, unpolished, with each stage labelled briefly.
React to a real comment or DM question. Quote it on screen, then answer with substance.

ideas.hook_formula: I challenged myself to {thing}. Here's day {N}.
ideas.body_template: Day-N-of-X format. Each entry is a mini update: what I did, what hurt, what I learned.

ideas.hook_formula: Save this for the next time {scenario}
ideas.body_template: Actionable reference card meant to be saved. One scenario on top, 3-5 bullet responses below. Caption tells them to tag a friend.

ideas.hook_formula: Tag someone who needs to hear this about {niche}
ideas.body_template: Sharing-native framing. The post's job is to earn a tag. One universal niche truth.
Introduce a (made-up or classic) mental framework. One slide = one letter / step. Memorable.

ideas.hook_formula: I'll say what no one else will: {confession}
ideas.body_template: Candid admission relevant to the niche. Single line, no hedging. Caption unpacks the why.

ideas.hook_formula: I was into {thing} before everyone else — and here's what they still don't get.
ideas.body_template: Expert-framed take. Establish history, then deliver the insight the newcomers miss.
Data-carousel. Each slide: one stat, one sentence of interpretation. Feels evidence-led.
Reframe a common niche complaint. Kind tone, surgical insight.
Invite the reader into an identity. Confirming statement + aspirational label.

ideas.hook_formula: The day {niche} started working for me
ideas.body_template: Narrative turning point. Set the stuck state, name the shift, show the after.

ideas.hook_formula: I failed at {thing}. Here's what I learned.
ideas.body_template: Failure post-mortem. 3-5 slides of concrete lessons, each with the specific detail that made it a lesson.
A real person mid-action in a niche-specific scene. Candid, unposed, natural lighting. The caption avoids describing the image and instead speaks to the feeling of the moment.
Close portrait of a niche persona, eye-level, neutral expression. The image carries the mood; the caption lands a single short truth.
A person in a real niche setting — working, practising, doing the unglamorous bit. Camera feels documentary, not posed.

ideas.hook_formula: What {niche_goal} would look like if nothing held you back
ideas.body_template: AI-visual reel with dreamlike, aspirational scenes tied to the niche. Hook promises the vision, scenes deliver 3-4 emotionally-loaded visuals, CTA lands the earthly next step.

ideas.hook_formula: The {niche} scene nobody gets to see
ideas.body_template: AI-generated visuals of the inner world / imagined scenes of the niche. Voiceover names what the viewer is seeing. 4-6 tightly paced scenes.

ideas.hook_formula: From what {thing} feels like to what it looks like
ideas.body_template: AI visual metaphor reel. Each scene is a visual translation of an internal niche experience. Voiceover bridges the felt sense to the visible.
Single-line wisdom that rewards re-reading. Attribution blank or the page. Short enough to fit a story in one glance.
A story quote posed as a provocative question. The byline credits 'you' so the audience sees the question as their own.
A gentle daily reminder tuned to the niche. The kind of line people screenshot and save to their own photos.
A single still image that visualises a niche emotion. Overlay text is optional and short. Lets the photo do the heavy lifting.
Proof-of-work story. Show the real, unfiltered artefact of today's niche work (training, build, cook, code). Overlay dates/times for texture.
A first-person-view photo representing a niche moment. Overlay is a short POV prompt. Keeps the audience inside the moment.

ideas.hook_formula: One quick {niche} tip before you scroll
ideas.body_template: ~8-12s story video: one concrete tip, voiceover direct to camera, single scene. Ends before the viewer can tap past.
A short voyeuristic clip that shows a sliver of niche practice. Minimal narration. Lets curiosity pull the viewer to the profile.
8-12s rant about a real niche frustration. Direct tone, one point, clear stop.
A photorealistic close portrait of a niche persona in the middle of an activity. Overlay text is ≤6 words so the face and environment carry the emotion.
A wider human shot inside a niche setting. Lets the environment establish the world. Minimal overlay — the scene is the story.
Candid vertical portrait — person mid-task. Overlay is a single niche truth that benefits from being set against a real face.
Tight detail shot of an under-shot part of niche practice. Caption names what we're looking at and why it matters.

ideas.hook_formula: {niche} as I live it — laid out
ideas.body_template: Flat-lay-style composition of niche essentials. Caption lists each item with one-line rationale.
